summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The TaskAttemptID class represents the immutable and unique identifier for a task attempt in Apache Hadoop MapReduce. It consists of two parts: the TaskID, which it belongs to, and the task attempt number.

CORE LOGIC:

The class provides methods to construct a TaskAttemptID object from a given TaskID or parts, as well as methods to retrieve the JobID, TaskID, and task type. It also provides methods to compare TaskAttemptIDs by their tipIds and task numbers.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The class provides methods to read and write TaskAttemptID objects to and from DataInput and DataOutput streams.

DEPENDENCIES:

The class depends on the JobID, TaskID, and ID classes from the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The TaskID class is a unique identifier for a Map or Reduce Task. It consists of three parts: the JobID, a character indicating whether the task is a map or reduce, and a task number.

CORE LOGIC:

The TaskID class provides methods for creating and parsing TaskID strings, as well as methods for comparing and hashing TaskIDs.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The TaskID class has methods for reading and writing TaskID objects to and from DataInput and DataOutput streams.

DEPENDENCIES:

The TaskID class depends on the JobID class and the TaskType enum.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This enum represents the different types of tasks that can be executed in a MapReduce job, such as map, reduce, job-setup, job-cleanup, and task-cleanup.

CORE LOGIC:
The enum itself does not contain any key algorithms or processing patterns.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The enum does not specify any inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
The enum does not depend on any other classes or packages.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where an application master (AM) has started.

CORE LOGIC:

The AMStartedEvent class contains the necessary information to record the start of an application master, including the application attempt ID, start time, container ID, node manager host, node manager port, and node manager HTTP port.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The AMStartedEvent class has methods to get the application attempt ID, start time, container ID, node manager host, node manager port, and node manager HTTP port.

DEPENDENCIES:

The AMStartedEvent class depends on the ApplicationAttemptId, ContainerId, and TimelineEvent classes from the Apache Hadoop YARN API.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- reads events from a file
- parses the events and creates the appropriate HistoryEvent object
- returns the next event from the stream

CORE LOGIC:
- uses the avro library to read and parse the events
- handles different event types and creates the appropriate HistoryEvent object

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- reads from a DataInputStream
- returns HistoryEvent objects

DEPENDENCIES:
- org.apache.avro.AvroRuntimeException
- org.apache.avro.Schema
- org.apache.avro.io.DatumReader
- org.apache.avro.io.Decoder
- org.apache.avro.io.DecoderFactory
- org.apache.avro.specific.SpecificData
- org.apache.avro.specific.SpecificDatumReader
- org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceAudience
- org.apache.hadoop.classification.InterfaceStability
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.FileSystem
- org.apache.hadoop.fs.Path
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.CounterGroup
- org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.Counters
- org.apache.hadoop.util.StringInterner

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

The EventWriter class is used to write events to the underlying stream. It typically creates one event writer per job.

CORE LOGIC:

The EventWriter class uses the Avro library to write events to the stream. It supports two encoding formats: JSON and BINARY.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The EventWriter class takes an FSDataOutputStream and a WriteMode as input, and it writes events to the stream.

DEPENDENCIES:

The EventWriter class depends on the org.apache.avro library for Avro encoding and decoding. It also depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package for the HistoryEvent and Event classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class is an interface for event wrapper classes. It wraps an Avro-generated class, adding constructors and accessor methods.

CORE LOGIC:

The class includes methods to get and set the Avro datum wrapped by the event, as well as methods to map the HistoryEvent to a TimelineEvent and retrieve any associated TimelineMetrics.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The class includes methods to get the event type and the Avro datum wrapped by the event.

DEPENDENCIES:

The class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.timelineservice.TimelineEvent and org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.timelineservice.TimelineMetric classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The HistoryEventHandler interface defines a method handleEvent that takes a HistoryEvent object as a parameter and throws an IOException.

CORE LOGIC:
The handleEvent method is the core logic of the HistoryEventHandler interface. It takes a HistoryEvent object as a parameter and processes it.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The handleEvent method takes a HistoryEvent object as a parameter and processes it. It does not have any inputs or outputs of its own.

DEPENDENCIES:
The HistoryEventHandler interface does not have any dependencies on other classes or packages.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The HistoryViewer class is used to parse and view the JobHistory files. It can print the job/task/attempt summary information in human-readable format or machine-readable JSON format.

CORE LOGIC:
The class uses the JobHistoryParser class to parse the history file and store the job information in the JobInfo object. It then uses the HistoryViewerPrinter class to print the job/task/attempt summary information in the specified format.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class takes the fully qualified Path of the History File and the Configuration file as inputs. It prints the job/task/attempt summary information to the PrintStream.

DEPENDENCIES:
The class depends on the JobHistoryParser, HistoryViewerPrinter, and other classes for parsing and printing the job history information.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The main purpose of this chunk of code is to print job history in different formats using the HistoryViewer.

CORE LOGIC:
The code does not contain any key algorithms or processing patterns visible in this chunk.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code does not contain any data flowing in or out.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the HistoryViewer class and the PrintStream class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code provides a way to print a human-readable summary of a job's history.

CORE LOGIC:
The code contains methods for printing job details, task summaries, job analysis, and task attempts.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes a JobHistoryParser.JobInfo object as input and prints the job history to a PrintStream.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the JobHistoryParser.JobInfo class and other classes in the same package.

---

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code defines two static Comparators for sorting TaskAttemptInfo objects based on different criteria.

CORE LOGIC:
The first Comparator, cFinishMapRed, sorts TaskAttemptInfo objects based on their finish time in descending order.
The second Comparator, cReduce, sorts TaskAttemptInfo objects based on the difference between their finish time and shuffle finish time in descending order.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code does not explicitly define any inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES:
This code depends on the JobHistoryParser class and its TaskAttemptInfo class.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is an event to record successful completion of a job.

CORE LOGIC:

The code sets the finish time, number of succeeded and failed maps and reduces, and the counters for the job.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes in the job ID, finish time, number of succeeded and failed maps and reduces, and the counters for the job as inputs. It outputs the job ID, finish time, number of succeeded and failed maps and reduces, and the counters for the job.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the JobID, Counters, and JobHistoryEventUtils classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
The JobHistoryParser class is responsible for parsing job history files and storing the parsed data in a data structure.

CORE LOGIC:
The code iterates over the values of a map of TaskInfo objects and calls the printAll() method on each object. The printAll() method prints various information about the task, including its ID, start time, finish time, type, split locations, and counters.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes in a map of TaskInfo objects as input and does not produce any output.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the existence of a TaskInfo class, which contains information about a particular task, and a TaskAttemptInfo class, which contains information about a particular task attempt. The code also depends on the existence of a Counters class, which is used to store performance counters for the job, task, or task attempt.

summary:
KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where the submit and launch time of a job are recorded.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this class is to store the job ID, submit time, and launch time in the JobInfoChange datum object.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this class are the job ID, submit time, and launch time. The outputs are the job ID, submit time, and launch time.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the JobID class from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package, the HistoryEvent interface, the JobInfoChange class, the TimelineEvent class, and the TimelineMetric class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where a job has been initialized. It contains information about the job, such as its ID, launch time, total number of maps, total number of reduces, job status, and whether the job's map and reduce stages were combined.

CORE LOGIC:

The class provides methods to get the job ID, launch time, total number of maps, total number of reduces, job status, and whether the job's map and reduce stages were combined.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The class takes in the job ID, launch time, total number of maps, total number of reduces, job status, and whether the job's map and reduce stages were combined as input parameters. It provides methods to get the job ID, launch time, total number of maps, total number of reduces, job status, and whether the job's map and reduce stages were combined as output.

DEPENDENCIES:

The class depends on the JobID class from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package, the TimelineEvent and TimelineMetric classes from the org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.timelineservice package, and the Utf8 class from the org.apache.avro.util package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is an event to record the change of priority of a job.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this chunk of code is to set the job ID and priority in the datum object.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The input to this chunk of code is the job ID and priority, and the output is the datum object.

DEPENDENCIES:

This chunk of code depends on the JobID and JobPriority classes from the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
- The main purpose of this chunk is to create a class that represents a job queue change event.

CORE LOGIC:
- This chunk does not contain any key algorithms or processing patterns.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
- The class has a constructor that takes a JobID and a queue name as input and sets them as the job ID and queue name for the event.
- The class also has methods to get the job ID and queue name from the event.

DEPENDENCIES:
- The class depends on the JobID class from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package and the TimelineEvent and TimelineMetric classes from the org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.timelineservice package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is an event to record the change of status for a job.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this chunk of code is to create an event that records the change in the job status.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this chunk of code are the Job ID and the new job status. The output is an event that records the change in the job status.

DEPENDENCIES:

This chunk of code depends on the JobID class and the HistoryEvent interface.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event of job unsuccessful completion.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this class is to record the completion status of a job, including whether it was successful, failed, or killed. It also records the number of successful and failed/killed maps and reduces, as well as the finish time of the job.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this class are the job ID, finish time, number of succeeded maps, number of succeeded reduces, number of failed maps, number of failed reduces, number of killed maps, number of killed reduces, and the status of the job. The outputs are the job ID, finish time, number of succeeded maps, number of succeeded reduces, number of failed maps, number of failed reduces, number of killed maps, number of killed reduces, and the status of the job.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the JobID class from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package, as well as the HistoryEvent and TimelineEvent classes from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY: NormalizedResourceEvent is an event to record the normalized map/reduce requirements.

CORE LOGIC: The event contains the normalized memory requirements and the task type of the request.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS: The event does not have any specific inputs or outputs.

DEPENDENCIES: The event depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and the TimelineEvent and TimelineMetric classes from the Apache Hadoop YARN API.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This chunk of code is a class named ReduceAttemptFinishedEvent. It is used to record the successful completion of a reduce attempt.

CORE LOGIC:
The class has a constructor that takes in various parameters such as the task attempt ID, task type, task status, shuffle finish time, sort finish time, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and an array of splits. It also has methods to get the task ID, attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time of the sort phase, finish time of the shuffle phase, finish time of the attempt, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and splits.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The class takes in input parameters such as task attempt ID, task type, task status, shuffle finish time, sort finish time, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and an array of splits. It also outputs the task ID, attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time of the sort phase, finish time of the shuffle phase, finish time of the attempt, hostname, port, rack name, state, counters, and splits.

DEPENDENCIES:
The class depends on the following classes: TaskAttemptID, TaskID, TaskType, Counters, ProgressSplitsBlock, SystemClock, TimelineEvent, TimelineMetric, and various utility classes for Avro and timeline metrics.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code represents an event to record successful task completion.

CORE LOGIC:
The code sets the task ID, attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time, rack name, hostname, state, and counters for the task attempt.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The code takes inputs such as the task attempt ID, task type, task status, finish time, rack name, hostname, state, and counters, and outputs a data object representing the task attempt finished event.

DEPENDENCIES:
The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and other classes and interfaces within that package. It also depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.Counters class for handling task attempt counters.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where a task attempt has started.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this class is to record the start of a task attempt.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this class are the task attempt ID, task type, start time, tracker name, HTTP port, shuffle port, container ID, locality, and avataar. The output is a record of the start of the task attempt.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the TaskAttemptID, TaskID, TaskType, and ContainerId classes from the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework. It also depends on the TimelineEvent and TimelineMetric classes from the Apache Hadoop YARN framework.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This class represents an event where a task attempt has failed or been killed.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this class is to record the unsuccessful completion of task attempts. It includes the task ID, task type, attempt ID, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, error, status, and counters.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this class are the task ID, task type, status, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, error, counters, and splits. The outputs are the task ID, task type, attempt ID, finish time, hostname, port, rack name, error, status, and counters.

DEPENDENCIES:

This class depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.util.JobHistoryEventUtils class.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is responsible for recording the failure of a task.

CORE LOGIC:

The code uses the provided data to create a TaskFailedEvent object, which includes the task ID, finish time, task type, error, status, failed attempt ID, and counters.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes in the necessary inputs to create a TaskFailedEvent object, including the task ID, finish time, task type, error, status, failed attempt ID, and counters. It then sets these inputs as the attributes of the TaskFailedEvent object.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package and its dependencies.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This chunk of code is responsible for recording the successful completion of a task. It includes the necessary attributes and methods to create and store the event data, as well as retrieve the relevant information.

CORE LOGIC:

The core logic of this code involves creating a TaskFinishedEvent object with the required parameters, such as task ID, successful task attempt ID, finish time, task type, status, and counters. The object is then serialized into an Avro data structure for storage or transmission.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The inputs to this code include the task ID, successful task attempt ID, finish time, task type, status, and counters. The outputs include the serialized TaskFinishedEvent object, which can be stored or transmitted.

DEPENDENCIES:

This code depends on the Apache Hadoop MapReduce framework and its associated classes and interfaces, such as TaskID, TaskAttemptID, TaskType, Counters, and HistoryEvent. It also depends on external libraries such as Avro and TimelineEvent.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This code creates an event to record the start of a task.

CORE LOGIC:

The code sets the task id, split locations, start time, and task type for the event.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code takes in the task id, start time, task type, and split locations as inputs and creates a TaskStartedEvent object.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code depends on the TaskID, TaskType, and HistoryEvent classes.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:

This code chunk is a Java class that represents an event to record updates to a task. It has a constructor that takes a TaskID and a finish time as parameters and sets the task ID and finish time in the datum object. It also has getter methods to retrieve the task ID and finish time.

CORE LOGIC:

The code chunk does not contain any key algorithms or processing patterns.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:

The code chunk takes a TaskID and a finish time as input parameters and sets them in the datum object. It also provides getter methods to retrieve the task ID and finish time.

DEPENDENCIES:

The code chunk depends on the TaskID class from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce package and the HistoryEvent interface from the org.apache.hadoop.mapreduce.jobhistory package. It also depends on the TimelineEvent and TimelineMetric classes from the org.apache.hadoop.yarn.api.records.timelineservice package.

KEY FUNCTIONALITY:
This code contains utility methods for converting Counters to JSON and to TimelineMetric.

CORE LOGIC:
The countersToJSON method converts the given Counters object to a JSON representation using the Jackson library.
The countersToTimelineMetric method converts the given Counters object to a set of TimelineMetric objects, with an optional group name prefix.

INPUTS/OUTPUTS:
The countersToJSON method takes a Counters object as input and returns a JSON representation of the counters.
The countersToTimelineMetric method takes a Counters object, a timestamp, and an optional group name prefix as input, and returns a set of TimelineMetric objects.

DEPENDENCIES:
This code depends on the Jackson library for JSON serialization and deserialization, as well as the TimelineMetric class from the yarn.api.records.timelineservice package.
